businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' company spanning autonomous driving, robotics, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY), net income $3.8B (-46.5% YoY). Wednesday close $401.12 (+0.47%). China February sales at 58,600 units (+91% YoY) and strong battery supplier earnings supported shares. Stock up 80% over past year despite revenue decline. Market cap $1.51T, P/E 371.24x, Forward P/E ~193x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack/Powerwall growth
Services/Other: ~10% of revenue. Supercharger network, insurance, parts
FSD/Software: ~$3B in license revenue. Subscription conversion accelerating

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on EPS $1.08. Extreme premium reflecting autonomous driving/robotics optionality

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026E EPS $2.07. Includes recovery expectations

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Sharp decline from price war + increased R&D spending

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects FY2025 profitability decline. FY2026E improvement expected

growthDrivers.title: China February Sales +91% YoY — 4th Consecutive Month of Growth
growthDrivers.description: Shanghai factory sold 58,600 Model 3/Y units, +91% YoY. Low base effect included, but 4 consecutive months of growth confirms China market recovery. Export numbers included.

growthDrivers.title: 80% Rally Over Past Year — Revaluation Despite Revenue Decline
growthDrivers.description: Stock rallied 80% despite FY2025 revenue decline and margin compression. Accelerating FSD software adoption and energy business expansion are the structural drivers.

growthDrivers.title: GTC 2026 (3/16-19) — Autonomous Driving Theme Catalyst
growthDrivers.description: NVIDIA's next-gen Rubin architecture reveal at GTC could spotlight next-gen AI chips for Tesla FSD, drawing renewed attention to the entire autonomous driving sector.

growthDrivers.title: Optimus Gen 3 Robot — New Wright's Law Growth Curve
growthDrivers.description: Seeking Alpha analysis: Optimus Gen 3 robotics ramp and long-term production efficiency gains could create a new growth curve offsetting near-term vehicle delivery declines.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market leader with 50%+ share, though globally surpassed by BYD. Vertical integration across software, manufacturing, energy, and 8.2B FSD miles is the core moat.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly more training data than competitors.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS as North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 2025 global EV sales of 2.26M units (+28%). Surpassed Tesla's 1.64M (-9%)

competitors.name: Lucid (LCID)
competitors.detail: Q4 revenue $523M (+123%). Targeting premium EV market

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 launch entering mass market. Intensifying price competition

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Expanding autonomous taxi service. L4 autonomy leader

recentNews.headline: China February EV Sales +91% YoY — Shanghai Factory 58,600 Units
recentNews.significance: 4th consecutive month of sales growth. Low base effect included but confirms China market recovery. Includes exports to Europe.

recentNews.headline: Trefis: 'How Tesla Stock Gained 80%' — Structural Analysis
recentNews.significance: Despite revenue decline and margin compression, high-margin software adoption and energy business expansion drove 80% rally.

recentNews.headline: Strong Battery Supplier Earnings Push TSLA to Intraday $405.80
recentNews.significance: Key battery supplier's strong results raised Tesla cost reduction expectations, driving 1.8% intraday surge.

recentNews.headline: BYD 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M — 5 Companies Eating Tesla's Lunch
recentNews.significance: 24/7 Wall St analyzes 5 competitors threatening Tesla. BYD's dominant growth is the biggest threat.

recentNews.headline: NHTSA FSD Safety Review Deadline (3/9) Triggers Stock Drop
recentNews.significance: FSD crash data investigation could result in fines, recalls, or robotaxi delays. Testing $400 support level.

analystOpinions.notableComment: Hold consensus among 30 analysts. Average target $396.15 (-1.24% from current). Extremely divided: high $600 (robotaxi success) vs low $125 (fundamentals-based).

risks.title: Global Deliveries Declining — Surpassed by BYD
risks.description: 2025 deliveries at 1.64M (-9% YoY). BYD delivered 2.26M (+28%), taking global #1. Further margin pressure from price competition.

risks.title: P/E 371x — Extreme Valuation
risks.description: P/E 371x despite revenue decline and margin compression. Autonomous driving and robotics success already priced in.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Safety Review Uncertainty
risks.description: Results pending after 3/9 deadline. Negative outcome could trigger recall, fines, or robotaxi delays.

risks.title: Executive Departures Continue — VP Finance Exits After 17 Years
risks.description: Sendil Palani VP Finance departure. Ongoing executive exit pattern raises organizational stability concerns.

risks.title: Tariff and Trade Policy Risks
risks.description: Shifting global trade policies and potential EV tariffs affecting China and European market access.

buyReasons.title: China February Sales +91% — Market Recovery Confirmed
buyReasons.rationale: 4 consecutive months of growth confirms China market bottom. Shanghai factory utilization improvement could drive margin recovery.

buyReasons.title: FSD/Robotaxi — $5T+ TAM Optionality
buyReasons.rationale: 8.2B FSD miles data. If NHTSA review passes, nationwide robotaxi expansion. Global mobility market $5T+ potential.

buyReasons.title: Energy Business +27% YoY — Hidden Growth Engine
buyReasons.rationale: Megapack/Powerwall generating $12.8B (+27% YoY). High-margin energy business partially offsetting vehicle revenue decline.

buyReasons.title: Net Cash $29.3B + FCF $6.22B — Strong Financial Position
buyReasons.rationale: Near debt-free balance sheet with ample R&D investment capacity.

overallOpinion: Tesla closed Wednesday at $401.12 (+0.47%), supported by China February sales surging 91% YoY and strong battery supplier earnings.

Despite the 80% rally over the past year, revenue declined 2.9% and operating margin compressed to 4.59%. The current price at P/E 371x largely prices in autonomous driving, robotics, and energy success.

Key catalysts are the NHTSA FSD safety review results and GTC 2026 (3/16-19). A positive FSD review could accelerate the robotaxi narrative; failure could trigger a sharp selloff. Trading near the 50-day SMA ($399.62), with 200-day SMA ($418.30) recovery as the key trend reversal signal.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.description: China February EV sales 58,600 units (+91% YoY)

threads.hook: China Feb sales +91% 🚀 Tesla recovery signal?
threads.text: Shanghai factory 58,600 units sold. 4 months of growth, closing at $401. But BYD sold 2.26M vs Tesla's 1.64M (-9%). The 80% rally is built on FSD and robotics narrative — it needs to prove out. 👉 investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-12

telegram.hook: 🚗 TSLA $401.12 (+0.47%) — China +91%, but the real catalyst is coming
telegram.text:
China February EV sales hit 58,600 units (+91% YoY), marking 4 consecutive months of growth. Battery supplier earnings added momentum.

But the real game-changer is next week: GTC 2026 (3/16) with NVIDIA Rubin reveal + NHTSA FSD review results imminent.

P/E 371x prices in autonomous driving and robotics success. Positive FSD review = robotaxi acceleration. Failure = sharp selloff. Barely holding above 50-day SMA ($399.62).

Full analysis 👉 investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-12
